articles.title: IMD forecasts heatwave conditions in several parts of country - News On AIR
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast heatwave conditions today in parts of Konkan and Goa, Madhya Pradesh, Marathwada, Saurashtra, Kutch, Uttar Pradesh, Vidarbha and West Rajasthan. Parts of West Madhya Pradesh are expected to experience warm night conditions today.
The weather agency also predicted heavy rainfall over Andaman and Nicobar Islands, north-east India Kerala, Mahe, South Interior Karnataka, Sub Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim and Tamil Nadu and Puducherry during the next 2-3 days.
Thunderstorms and lightning are also expected today in places over Andhra Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Arunachal Pradesh, Gangetic West Bengal and Odisha.

articles.title: Low pressure area forming over Bay of Bengal; monsoon to arrive in Andaman, heatwave grips north India - CNBC TV18
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Andaman and Nicobar Islands
articles.full_text:
3 Min Read
The southwest monsoon is likely to make its onset over parts of the south Bay of Bengal, the Andaman Sea and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands around May 16, even as a low-pressure area persists over the Bay of Bengal and heatwave conditions continue to intensify across several parts of north and central India, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) said.
A well-marked low-pressure area over the southwest and adjoining west-central Bay of Bengal remained active on Thursday, May 14, accompanied by a cyclonic circulation extending up to 5.8 km above mean sea level, the IMD said in its weather bulletin.
The IMD has advised fishermen not to venture into several parts of the Bay of Bengal, the Andaman Sea and areas along the Kerala coast due to rough sea conditions expected between May 15 and 19.
Severe heatwave conditions across several states
The IMD has forecast heatwave to severe heatwave conditions over northwest India during the coming week.

West Rajasthan is expected to witness severe heatwave conditions in isolated pockets between May 17 and 19, while heatwave conditions are likely over Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Vidarbha, Chhattisgarh and Telangana on multiple days between May 15 and 20.
Maximum temperatures may rise by 3-5 degrees Celsius over northwest India till May 17.
Jaisalmer in Rajasthan recorded the country’s highest maximum temperature at 46.1 degrees Celsius on May 13.
The weather department also said warm night conditions are likely over parts of West Madhya Pradesh and Vidarbha, with scattered warm night conditions expected over West Rajasthan later in the week.
Heavy rainfall forecast in northeast and south India
The IMD has predicted isolated heavy to very heavy rainfall over parts of northeast India over the next several days.

Assam and Meghalaya are likely to receive very heavy rainfall on May 18, while heavy rainfall activity is expected across Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura till May 20.
In southern India, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry, Kerala and South Interior Karnataka are expected to receive isolated heavy rainfall between May 15 and 19.
Thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds reaching up to 50 kmph are also likely across Kerala, Karnataka, Rayalaseema and coastal Andhra Pradesh.
Delhi NCR weather forecast
For Delhi-NCR, the IMD forecast mainly clear to partly cloudy skies between May 15 and 17.
Maximum temperatures are expected to remain between 40-42 degrees Celsius, while minimum temperatures may range from 23-26 degrees Celsius.

A well-marked low-pressure area over the southwest and adjoining west-central Bay of Bengal remained active on Thursday, May 14, accompanied by a cyclonic circulation extending up to 5.8 km above mean sea level, the IMD said in its weather bulletin.
It added that conditions were favourable for the advance of the monsoon into the Andaman region over the next two days.
The IMD has advised fishermen not to venture into several parts of the Bay of Bengal, the Andaman Sea and areas along the Kerala coast due to rough sea conditions expected between May 15 and 19.
Severe heatwave conditions across several states
The IMD has forecast heatwave to severe heatwave conditions over northwest India during the coming week.
